entities.visual: Self-possessed English girl of fifteen, Edwardian afternoon dress, calm knowing eyes.
Nervous English gentleman in his thirties, pale, formal visiting clothes, stick and hat clutched close.
Brisk cheerful middle-aged Englishwoman, country tweeds, eyes always straying to the window.
Hearty English country squire in a white waterproof mackintosh, gun under arm, muddy boots.
Practical Englishwoman, Framton's sister, letter-writing desk and firm expression.
Hearty young Englishman, youngest brother, gun over shoulder, teasing grin.

narrator.design: Calm, measured middle-aged male storyteller; low, warm register; dry wit; unhurried delivery.

vera.design: English girl of fifteen; clear, poised, articulate voice; deceptively calm and self-possessed.

framton.design: Nervous English gentleman in his thirties; thin, hesitant, anxious voice; slightly breathless.

mrs_sappleton.design: Cheerful middle-aged Englishwoman; bright, brisk, sociable voice; easily distracted.

mr_sappleton.design: Hearty English country squire; jovial, booming, weathered voice.

sister.design: Practical middle-aged Englishwoman; firm, brisk, no-nonsense voice.

ronnie.design: Hearty young Englishman; bright sing-song teasing voice.

hints.match: I know how it will be
you will bury yourself down there
Out through that window, three years ago
The doctors agree in ordering me
I said, Bertie, why do you bound
I hope Vera has been amusing you
fairly muddy, but most of it's dry
I expect it was the spaniel

scenes.anchor: "My aunt will be down presently, Mr. Nuttel,"
scenes.visual: Comfortable Edwardian country sitting room on an October afternoon, large French window open onto a lawn, a poised girl of fifteen entertaining a nervous visitor.

scenes.anchor: "Out through that window, three years ago to a day
scenes.visual: The same room, hushed; the girl gazes at the open window telling a ghost story, the visitor leaning in, chilled.

scenes.anchor: "I hope Vera has been amusing you?"
scenes.visual: The aunt bustles in with cheerful apologies, tea things, the visitor pale and uneasy between them.
Three figures with guns coming through the French window out of the twilight, a tired brown spaniel at heel, the visitor's chair empty.

cues.anchor: In the deepening twilight three figures
the gravel-drive, and the front gate
